observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor and see to it that net take home pay of employees shall not be less than the required minimum monthly net take home pay pursuant to the provisions of the General Appropriations Act for the year. Also, any deficiency in loan payments and other obligations as a result of the mandatory minimum requirement may be addressed through direct payment arrangements to be voluntarily made by the employees to the concerned institutions.
observations.reason:
Ongoing Implementation.
The Audit Team has issued an updated AOM regarding the Observation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive ensure that the target schedules of implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) are met;
b. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund; and
c. The Municipal Engineer facilitate the timely implementation of the development projects and diligently supervise its completion in order to provide socio-economic development and environmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries.
observations.reason:
On-going Implementation.
Planning for programs, projects and activities is ongoing.
There are projects under the 20% Development Fund that are still unimplemented.
Reiterated in CY 2021 AAR, Part II, Observation No. 4.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
a. Transfer appropriate funds for the titling of the land; The agency shall appropriate funds for the titling of untitled lots maintained by Municipality and the processing of land titling shall be facilitated by the Municipal Assessor’s Office.
b. The Municipal Assessor’s Office be directed to exert effort on titling all parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government; and
c. The Accounting Office should facilitate the booking up of the real properties whenever the document is available.
observations.reason: The process of land titling is ongoing and it is being facilitated by the Municipal Assessor’s Office.

observations.recommendation: 1.5 We recommended and the Management agreed that the concerned Municipal Officials facilitate full liquidation of fund transfers from other Government Agencies in accordance with the agreements entered thereto.

2.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive, Municipal Planning and Development
Office and the Office of the Municipal Engineer strictly exercise proper
planning, timely monitoring and implementation of the projects; and
b. The Municipality strictly adhere with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 1 in planning and implementing programs,
projects and activities.

3.7 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor and see to it that monthly net take home pay of employees shall not be less than the required minimum monthly net take home pay pursuant to the provisions of the General Appropriations Act for the year. Also, we recommended and the Management agreed that any deficiency in loan payments and other obligations as a result of the mandatory minimum requirement may be addressed through direct payment arrangements to be voluntarily made by the employees to the concerned institutions.

4.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare accurate quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization and ensure that the Local School Board (LSB) submits these reports to the DepEd and the Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, in compliance with Joint Circular No. 2017-01.

5.6 We recommended and the Management agreed that Box A of the Disbursement Vouchers for disbursements charged against the Special Education Fund be duly signed by the Department of Education District Supervisor in their capacity as Co-Chairman of the Local School Board in compliance with applicable regulations.

6.5 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Local School Board to prepare a 3-year program that outlines strategic prioritization policies for allocating the Municipality’s Special Education Fund (SEF), in compliance with Section 5.1 of Joint Circular No. 2017-01.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notices to the payees one month before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation;
b. For improperly reported stale checks, require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to provide evidence that the checks are not outstanding that they have been voided and no obligation exist; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for stale checks.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the Management agreed to:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE secures the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the utilization of the lump sum appropriation appropriated under the 20% development fund.
We further recommended and the Management also agreed that the MBO will exercise prudence in the preparation of the executive budget and comply with the requirements by the Budget Operations Manual prior to submission to the Sanggunian for authorization through enactment of Appropriation Ordinance.

observations.managementAction: We recommended and the Management agreed to:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require all the Department Heads to submit the POs with other supporting documents within the prescribed period and strictly adhere with Section 3 of COA Circular 2009-01.
We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the prompt liquidation of the cash advances and withhold the salary for non-liquidation if warranted.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive ensure that the target schedules of implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) are met;

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund; and 

The Municipal Engineer facilitate the timely implementation of the development projects and diligently supervise its completion in order to provide socio-economic development and environmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive ensure that the target schedules of implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) are met;

The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator carefully plan the programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% development fund; and 

The Municipal Engineer facilitate the timely implementation of the development projects and diligently supervise its completion in order to provide socio-economic development and environmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended the following:

The Agency appropriate funds for the titling of the land;

The Municipal Assessor’s Office be directed to exert effort on titling all parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government; and 

The Accounting Office should facilitate the booking up of the real properties whenever the document is available.

1.6	We recommended and the Management agreed to:
1.	Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notices to the payees one month before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation;
2.	For improperly reported stale checks, require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to provide evidence that the checks are not outstanding that they have been voided and no obligation exist; and
3.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for stale checks.

We recommended that the Management: 
Discontinue the practice of erroneously charging maintenance and other operating expenses of the LDRRMO to the LDRRMF.

Revert to the LDRRM Fund the MOOE of the LDRRMO totaling P1.072 million in CY 2022 that were erroneously charged against the fund which shall be re-appropriated to finance only those programs, projects and activities identified to be funded under the 5% LDRRM Fund for the ensuing year.

Appropriate funds for the MOOE of the LDRRMO and ensure that the disbursements charged against the LDRRMF are strictly in accordance with the NDRRMC-DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and attach the Statement of Time Elapsed and Percentage of Work Accomplished to every progress billing of infrastructure projects.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the winning suppliers or their duly authorized representatives to clearly indicate in the purchase orders their date of receipt thereof.

We recommended that the OIC – Accounting and Internal Audit Services Office revert to the general fund the accounts payable which have remained outstanding for at least two years and against which no actual claim, administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not covered by perfected contracts on record.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive ensure that the target schedules of implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) are met;
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund; and
The Municipal Engineer facilitate the timely implementation of the development projects and diligently supervise its completion in order to provide socio-economic development and environmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended the following:

The Accounting Office correct their practice of treating welfare goods received as an outright expense by recording these procured inventory items to the Welfare Goods for Distribution account for proper recognition and measurement; and

The Accounting Office and the Supply and Property Unit maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, to properly monitor and reconcile the welfare goods received and issued.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive require concerned personnel or committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year and reflect the result in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI); and 

The Accounting Office and the Supply and Property Unit maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively and periodically reconcile their balances for accurate presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure the technical specifications of the items to be procured are specific and detailed to properly determine the reasonableness of the price and the propriety of the goods delivered.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive ensure that the target schedules of implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) are met;


The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator carefully plan the programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% development fund; and 

The Municipal Engineer facilitate the timely implementation of the development projects and diligently supervise its completion in order to provide socio-economic development and environmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA 10121.

We recommended the following:

The Agency appropriate funds for the titling of the land (Fully Implemented in CY 2019);

The Municipal Assessor’s Office be directed to exert effort on titling all parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government (Fully Implemented in CY 2019); and 

The Accounting Office should facilitate the booking up of the real properties whenever the document is available.

We recommended the following and Management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive ensure that the target schedules of implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) are met;
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator carefully plan the programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% development fund; and
The Municipal Engineer facilitate the timely implementation of the development projects and diligently supervise its completion in order to provide socio-economic development and environmental benefits for the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017; and

For the generated savings and unimplemented projects which are inconsistent with the governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments or realignment that will address the needs of the constituents.

We recommended the following:

The Agency appropriate funds for the titling of the land (Fully Implemented in CY 2019);

The Municipal Assessor’s Office be directed to exert effort on titling all parcels of land owned by the Municipal Government (Fully Implemented in CY 2019); and 

The Accounting Office should facilitate the booking up of the real properties whenever the document is available.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA 10121.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA 10121.

For the generated savings and unimplemented projects which are inconsistent with the governing rules and regulations, initiate amendments or realignment that will address the needs of the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipality exert extra effort for the perfection of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and the completion of the required water permit from the Water Resource Board (NWRB) for the immediate implementation of the project.

We recommended that additional training courses for personnel and the involvement of community based DRRM shall be included in the Municipality’s LDRRM Plan as required under Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 2012 and Section 2 of Rule 10 of the Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR) of Republic Act 10121.

We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board through the Solid Waste Management Focal Person follow up the status of the approval of the plan as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.

We recommended that the Municipality update its Gender and Development (GAD) database for gender statistics and sex-disaggregation data as required under Republic Act (RA) 9710 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR), Memorandum Circular No. 2014-05 of the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) and DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2013-01.

We recommended the following:


The Municipal Treasurer/Project-In-Charge continue to exert extra effort in ensuring collection not only for SEA loan but also other long and overdue account receivables that has been carried in the books of the Municipality. 

For those account that can no longer be collected, the Head of the government entity may request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to reduce the balance of a long and overdue receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer/Project-In-charge exerts effort in ensuring collection of the said loans. Also secure the death certificates of the deceased beneficiary and request from COA write-off of the accounts.

For the KKK account, the Municipal Accountant exhausts all possible remedies to locate its subsidiary ledger or record, beneficiary’s physical existence and demand collection.  If she found it fruitless, request for write- off of the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction:
Collected P10, 400 under SEAP loan and P 8,500 for KKK fund with remaining balance of P48, 244.35 and P 76,000 respectively as of December 31, 2018.

Municipal treasurer was able to collect/secure copy of death certificate of some recipient who does no longer exist to be supported to the request for write off.

Reiterated as Finding No. 3 AAR 2015

Reiterated as Finding No. 6 AAR 2016

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated supply officer/treasurer to conduct an inventory of all their buildings, structures and other insurable assets and insure them with GSIS as mandated in COA Circular No. 79-112 dated August 30, 1979, if finances permit.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant request for the writing off of the past due loan accounts in accordance with Section 5, Rule VI of the 2009 Revised Rules and
 Procedures of the Commission on Audit.

Required documents to support the request for the writing off were not yet accomplished due to the absence of data to support the brief history of the loans.

We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% Development fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects and activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
Regular monitoring mechanisms be employed in the implementation of the 20% development projects and a closer coordination among officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be enhanced to achieve the desired level of efficiency. We recommended the following:
The Municipal Treasurer/Project-In-charge exert effort in ensuring collection of the said loans. Also secure the death certificates of the deceased beneficiary and request from COA write-off of the accounts.
For the KKK account, the Municipal Accountant exhausts all possible remedies to locate its subsidiary ledger or record, beneficiary’s physical existence and demand collection.  If she found it fruitless, request for write- off of the accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the inventory committee conduct the periodic physical inventory of all its property and equipment owned by the LGU and prepare RPCPPE to be used by the Municipal Accountant as basis for booking up of unrecorded PPE.  Also, reconciliation of PPE accounts with accounting records shall also be conducted to determine the necessary adjustments to be made and to ensure more reliable account balance of the PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated supply officer/treasurer to conduct an inventory of all their buildings, structures and other insurable assets and insure them with GSIS as mandated in COA Circular No. 79-112 dated August 30, 1979, if finances permit.
observations.managementAction: The municipality had already allocated or appropriated the amount for insurance in CY 2018 and the application   for insurance with the GSIS is under process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.  The Municipal Accountant send demand letter to personnel requiring them immediate liquidation of their cash advances especially those, wherein the prescribed period to liquidate had already lapsed.

We recommended that the Municipal SWM Board through the MENRO and the SWM Focal Person hasten the immediate submission to the National Solid Waste Management Commission of the Waste Analysis and Characterization Survey (WACS) as required under Section 17 (b) of RA 9003 and NSWMC Resolution No. 362 series of 2016 for the timely approval of the LGUs Solid Waste Management Plan in conformity with Section 16 of the said RA 9003.
observations.managementAction:
Revised Solid Waste Management Plan already submitted for approval/deliberation

 Finding was first raised in AAR 2014 as Finding No. 6

observations.recommendation: We recommend that procurement out of MDRRM Fund be taken up as Inventory. We also recommend that Supplies Ledger Cards be maintained by the Accounting Unit and Stock Cards by the Property Section to achieve better quality financial reports and availability of information in the receipt and utilization of MDRRM Funds allocated from the agency’s regular budget for DRRM program.

We recommend that unserviceable properties be properly disposed of in accordance with COA Circular No. 89-296 dated January 27, 1989.
We also recommend that obsolete and non-functioning assets be reclassified from Property, Plant and Equipment to Other Assets account.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer regularly renew the Memorandum Receipt for Equipment using the new form – Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) issued to the concerned officials and employees every three (3) years or every time there is a new custodian of such property or equipment.

We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer should regularly conduct tax information campaign for delinquent taxpayers. The Municipal Treasurer should coordinate regularly with the barangay officials and schedule a visit to each of the 50 barangays of the Municipality to intensify their tax collection/campaign.

We recommend that the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer consolidate APP’s of all requisitioning offices which will serve as basis of the procurement program of the agency. Bulk purchase thru public bidding is encouraged to generate savings and facilitate processing of transactions.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant request for the writing off of the past due loan accounts in accordance with Section 5, Rule VI of the 2009 Revised Rules and
 Procedures of the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason:
Required documents to support the request for the writing off were not yet accomplished due to the absence of data  to support the brief history of the loans.

Finding was first raised in AAR 2014 as Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the inventory committee conduct the periodic physical inventory of all its property and equipment owned by the LGU and  prepare RPCPPE to be used by the Municipal Accountant as basis for booking up of unrecorded PPE.  Also, reconciliation of PPE accounts with accounting records shall also be conducted to determine the necessary adjustments to be made and to ensure more reliable account balance of the PPE in the financial statements.

We  recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the designated supply officer/treasurer to conduct an inventory of all their buildings, structures and other insurable assets and  insure them with GSIS  as mandated in COA Circular No. 79-112 dated August 30, 1979, if finances permit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.  The  Municipal Accountant send demand letter to personnel requiring them immediate liquidation of their cash advances especially those, wherein  the prescribed period to liquidate had already lapsed.

We recommended that the Municipal SWM Board through the MENRO and the SWM Focal Person hasten the immediate submission to the National Solid Waste Management Commission  of the  Waste Analysis  and Characterization Survey (WACS) as required under Section 17 (b) of RA 9003 and NSWMC Resolution No. 362 series of 2016 for the timely approval of the LGUs Solid Waste Management Plan in conformity with Section 16 of the said RA 9003.

We recommended that regular monitoring mechanisms be employed in  the implementation of the 20% development projects and a closer coordination among the officials involved in planning, execution and monitoring be enhanced to achieve the desired level of efficiency.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer / Project In-charge exert effort in ensuring collection of the said loans.  Also secure the death certificates of the two deceased beneficiaries and request from COA the write-off of these receivables.

We recommended that accounting and property records be reconciled in accordance with COA Circular No. 91-368 and Section 114 of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs to ensure reliability and accuracy of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason:
Partial inventory of property was conducted as of December 31, 2016

Reconciliation is on-going.  

Finding was first raised in AAR 2013  as Finding No. 2
